questions.question: Derive the expression for work done when a force is applied at an angle to the displacement.
questions.answer: Work done W = F × d × cosθ, where θ is the angle between force and displacement. Only the component of force along the displacement does work.

questions.question: A body of mass 3 kg is moving at 6 m/s. Calculate its kinetic energy and work required to double its speed.
questions.answer: Initial KE = 1/2 × 3 × 6² = 54 J. Final KE = 1/2 × 3 × 12² = 216 J. Work required = ΔKE = 216 – 54 = 162 J

questions.question: Explain with examples how work can be positive, negative, or zero.
questions.answer: Positive work: force and displacement same direction (pushing a box). Negative work: force opposite displacement (friction). Zero work: displacement perpendicular to force (holding a book horizontally).

questions.question: Derive the formula for kinetic energy using work-energy theorem.
questions.answer: Work done W = ΔKE. If a constant force F accelerates mass m over distance d: W = F × d = m × a × d. Using v² = u² + 2ad and u=0 → W = 1/2 mv² = KE.

questions.question: A block of mass 4 kg is lifted vertically by 2 m. Calculate work done and potential energy gained.
questions.answer: PE = mgh = 4 × 9.8 × 2 = 78.4 J. Work done W = PE = 78.4 J

questions.question: State and explain the law of conservation of energy with a practical example.
questions.answer: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed. Total energy in an isolated system remains constant. Example: Pendulum converts PE to KE and vice versa.

questions.question: A car of mass 1000 kg moving at 20 m/s brakes to stop. Calculate the work done by brakes.
questions.answer: Initial KE = 1/2 × 1000 × 20² = 200,000 J. Final KE = 0. Work done by brakes = ΔKE = 200,000 – 0 = 200,000 J

questions.question: Explain how a pendulum demonstrates conversion between kinetic and potential energy.
questions.answer: At topmost point: PE max, KE=0. At bottom: KE max, PE=0. Energy continuously converts between KE and PE during oscillation.

questions.question: Derive the relation between work done and change in potential energy when lifting a body vertically.
questions.answer: Work done W = F × h. Force along vertical = mg, displacement = h → W = mgh = ΔPE.

questions.question: Explain the difference between kinetic and potential energy with examples.
questions.answer: Kinetic energy: energy of motion (moving car). Potential energy: energy of position (water at height).

questions.question: A spring of constant k = 200 N/m is stretched by 0.1 m. Calculate elastic potential energy stored.
State and explain work-energy theorem with an example.
questions.answer: Work done on a body = change in KE, W = ΔKE. Example: A force accelerates a block from rest to 5 m/s → KE = 1/2 mv².

questions.question: Define power and derive expression using work done and time.
questions.answer: Power is rate of doing work, P = W / t. Derived: W = F × d → P = F × d / t = F × v (if motion uniform).

questions.question: A machine does 600 J of work in 30 s. Calculate power.
Explain difference between conservative and non-conservative forces with examples.
questions.answer: Conservative: path-independent, energy conserved (gravity, spring). Non-conservative: path-dependent, dissipates energy (friction, air resistance).

questions.question: A block of 2 kg slides down a 5 m high frictionless incline. Find speed at bottom.
questions.answer: PE_top = KE_bottom → mgh = 1/2 mv² → v = √(2gh) = √(2 × 9.8 × 5) ≈ 9.9 m/s

questions.question: Explain how efficiency of a machine is calculated with formula.
questions.answer: Efficiency = (Useful work output / Total work input) × 100%

questions.question: A machine does 450 J of useful work using 600 J of input. Find efficiency.
Explain different forms of energy with examples.
questions.answer: Mechanical (KE & PE), Thermal (heat), Chemical (battery/fuel), Electrical, Nuclear. Energy can be converted from one form to another.

questions.question: Explain how a pendulum demonstrates energy transformation and conservation.
questions.answer: At highest point: PE max, KE=0. At lowest: KE max, PE=0. Total mechanical energy remains constant (ignoring friction).

questions.question: Explain why work can be zero even when force is applied.
questions.answer: If displacement is zero or force is perpendicular to displacement, W = F × d × cosθ = 0.

questions.question: A 5 kg mass is lifted to 8 m. Calculate work done against gravity and PE gained.
Explain with example how energy is dissipated by non-conservative forces.
questions.answer: Friction converts mechanical energy into heat, reducing KE and PE of the system. Example: sliding block slows down due to friction.

questions.question: Explain difference between power and energy with examples.
questions.answer: Energy: capacity to do work (battery stores 50 J). Power: rate of doing work (motor does 50 J in 5 s → P=10 W).

questions.question: Explain how renewable and non-renewable energy sources affect daily energy use and work done.
questions.answer: Renewable: solar, wind provide sustainable energy. Non-renewable: coal, petrol deplete resources. Energy availability affects work output and efficiency in machines.
